title: Chapter 1: Greetings & Basics
description: Learn to say hello, goodbye, and basic introductions.

title: Chapter 2: Locations
description: Express where things and people are using 'estar'.

title: Chapter 3: Descriptions
description: Describe objects and people using 'ser' and adjectives.

title: Chapter 4: Possession
description: Talk about what you have using 'tener'.

title: Chapter 5: Regular -AR Verbs
description: Learn basic actions like speaking, working, and studying.

title: Chapter 6: Regular -ER/-IR Verbs
description: Action verbs like eating, drinking, living, and opening.

title: Chapter 7: Reflexive Verbs
description: Daily routines and actions done to oneself.

title: Chapter 8: Expressing Likes
description: Use 'gustar' to express what you like.

title: Chapter 9: The Near Future
description: Express what you are going to do using 'ir + a'.

title: Chapter 10: The Preterite Tense
description: Talk about completed past actions.

title: Chapter 11: The Imperfect Tense
description: Describe ongoing past actions and habits.

title: Chapter 12: The Present Perfect
description: Talk about what you 'have done'.

title: Chapter 13: Direct Object Pronouns
description: Replace nouns with 'lo, la, los, las'.

title: Chapter 14: Indirect Object Pronouns
description: Express 'to whom' or 'for whom' using 'le' and 'les'.

title: Chapter 15: Double Object Pronouns
description: Use both direct and indirect object pronouns together.

title: Chapter 16: Affirmative Commands
description: Give direct orders like 'Speak' or 'Eat'.

title: Chapter 17: Negative Commands
description: Learn to say 'Don't do' something.

title: Chapter 18: Subjunctive: Desires
description: Express what you want others to do using 'Quiero que'.

title: Chapter 19: Subjunctive: Hope
description: Express hope and uncertainty with 'Espero que'.

title: Chapter 20: Subjunctive: Importance
description: Use impersonal expressions like 'Es importante que'.

title: Chapter 21: Real Conditionals
description: Talk about what happens if a condition is met.

title: Chapter 22: Hypothetical Conditionals
description: Talk about what would happen in hypothetical situations.

title: Chapter 23: Done Actions (Acabar de)
description: Say what you just finished doing.

title: Chapter 24: Duration (Llevar + Gerund)
description: Express how long you've been doing something.

title: Caperucita Roja - Part 1
description: A classic fairy tale told in Spanish.

title: Don Quijote de la Mancha - Excerpt
description: A passage from the most famous work of Spanish literature.

title: Poetry: Bécquer
description: Rimas by Gustavo Adolfo Bécquer.

title: Grammar: The Past Subjunctive
description: Practice the imperfect subjunctive for hypothetical situations.

title: Theme: Travel and Airport
description: Useful vocabulary and phrases for traveling by plane.

title: Theme: Business and Office
description: Vocabulary for professional settings and meetings.

title: Theme: Food and Dining
description: Vocabulary for restaurants, cooking, and food.

title: Theme: Health and Medicine
description: Vocabulary for doctor visits, symptoms, and pharmacy.

title: Theme: Nature and Environment
description: Vocabulary for weather, landscapes, and conservation.

title: Poetry: Mariposa del Aire
description: A lovely poem by Federico García Lorca.

title: Dialogue: At the Café
description: A conversation between two friends ordering coffee.

title: Literature: Cien Años de Soledad
description: The iconic opening of Gabriel García Márquez's masterpiece.
